paragraphs.text: Short answer: “The” binding constant for DTPA depends on which metal you mean. In SRD‑46, DTPA (ligand_6356) has measured overall 1:1 formation constants for the equilibrium [M] + [L] ⇌ [ML] (β1, typically reported as logβ1) with many metals in aqueous solution. Most entries are near 25 °C and ionic strength ≈ 0.1 M.

claims.text: “The” binding constant for DTPA depends on which metal you mean.

paragraphs.text:
Notes and how to proceed
- Definition used: β1 (or logβ1) for [M] + [L] ⇌ [ML] in water (beta_def_812 in SRD‑46).
- Conditions matter: values differ with temperature and ionic strength; I’ve listed the closest-to-25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M entries SRD‑46 indicates are available for DTPA.
- Practical insight: DTPA is a strong multidentate chelator and typically binds trivalent and tetravalent metals more strongly than many divalent ions, which is why DTPA derivatives are widely used for lanthanides/actinides (e.g., Gd3+).
